summary:
Daily work prep briefing — Saturday, March 14

Schedule
I’m not seeing any meetings on your calendar for today. The only recent calendar item surfaced was yesterday’s 30-minute meeting with Sagiv and external attendees.

You also received an automated task update this morning saying “No meetings or actions today.”

What matters most today
Your clearest active priorities still appear to be:

1) Solutions / ERP roadmap — confirm engineering capacity and release vehicle for the foundational template, and get engineering sizing on the admin configuration work.

2) Market — prepare for the broader product-team review of the multi-domain/brand PRD on Thursday, March 18, with a plan to finish sizing and Jira handoff by April 1.

3) Bandwidth risk management — your latest update flags engineering bandwidth as the main risk on the Solutions / ICP / ERP lanes.

Fresh internal signals from today / late yesterday
- Confluence digest: Cornelia Berchy and Brett Christenson updated “Weekly Review - PRD, Design and UX Copy.”
- Slack: Ben Hall posted in #si-releases that ProdAU will not have Nylas because there is no Australian region, and that this had already been agreed.
- Security: Okta flagged a new sign-on to your account from Chrome on Linux in Santa Clara, CA. If that wasn’t you, that is the most urgent thing in your inbox.

Likely prep items for next working block
- Tighten the ask and owner list for engineering-capacity decisions on the Solutions / ERP lane.
- Make sure the multi-domain/brand PRD review for March 18 has the right framing, decision points, and dependencies called out.
- Review the updated PRD / Design / UX Copy page before Monday so you’re current on product/design wording changes.

Risks / watchouts
- Main strategic risk remains staffing/bandwidth on the Solutions and ERP-related workstreams.
- Slack note about ProdAU / Nylas may matter if any release or regional assumptions were still floating around.

Bottom line
Today looks like a low-meeting, high-prep…
